name: calc
description: Spreadsheet creation, format conversion (ODS/XLSX/CSV), formulas, data automation with LibreOffice Calc.
qualityReason: Useful but overlaps with general tools
capabilities: Create new ODS spreadsheets from scratch using Python ezodf library
Convert between ODS, XLSX, CSV, PDF, and HTML formats using soffice CLI
Automate formulas and calculations using LibreOffice UNO API
Batch convert multiple spreadsheet files in one operation
Create pivot tables, charts, and data validation rules
Import and export data from databases and APIs
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate LibreOffice Calc documentation and automation skill. All static findings are false positives. The skill contains documentation for spreadsheet creation, format conversion (ODS/XLSX/CSV/PDF), and data automation using LibreOffice command-line tools and Python UNO API. No malicious patterns found.
valueStatement: Automate spreadsheet workflows with LibreOffice Calc. This skill enables creating, converting, and processing ODS, XLSX, CSV, and PDF files through command-line tools and Python scripting.
userTitle: Create and Convert Spreadsheets with LibreOffice Calc
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installed on the system
Python UNO API requires a running LibreOffice instance in server mode
Complex formatting may not perfectly transfer between formats
Some advanced Excel features may not be fully compatible

useCases.targetUser: Data analysts who need recurring spreadsheet reports
useCases.title: Data Analyst Reporting
useCases.description: Automate weekly report generation by converting raw CSV data into formatted ODS spreadsheets with pivot tables and charts.

useCases.title: System Integration
useCases.description: Integrate spreadsheet operations into larger workflows by converting between formats and automating data imports from external sources.

useCases.title: Document Conversion Service
useCases.description: Batch convert legacy spreadsheet files to modern formats for archival or sharing purposes.

promptTemplates.title: Create Simple Spreadsheet
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new ODS spreadsheet called 'report.ods' with columns for Name, Date, and Amount. Add 3 sample rows of data.

promptTemplates.title: Convert File Format
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert the spreadsheet 'data.ods' to XLSX format using LibreOffice Calc command-line tools.

promptTemplates.title: Add Formulas
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a spreadsheet with columns A (Product), B (Quantity), C (Price). Add a formula in column D that calculates Quantity times Price.

promptTemplates.title: Batch Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a bash script that batch converts all ODS files in the current directory to XLSX format using soffice.

bestPractices: Use ODS as the master format for source files to preserve all features
Test conversions with sample files before batch processing
Use headless mode (--headless) for server-side automation
Store Python scripts with UNO connections for complex operations
antiPatterns: Converting files with macros to PDF (macros are not preserved)
Using XLSX as master format (some ODS features may be lost)
Running multiple soffice instances simultaneously (causes conflicts)
Not backing up original files before batch conversions

faq.question: What is the difference between ODS and XLSX?
faq.answer: ODS is the native OpenDocument Spreadsheet format. XLSX is Microsoft's Excel format. ODS is open source, while XLSX has better compatibility with Excel but may lose some advanced formatting.

faq.question: Do I need LibreOffice installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, LibreOffice must be installed on the system. The skill uses the 'soffice' command-line tool and Python UNO API which require LibreOffice.

faq.question: Can this skill work with password-protected files?
faq.answer: The command-line tools have limited support for password-protected files. You may need to remove protection before conversion or use the GUI version.

faq.question: How do I automate LibreOffice with Python?
faq.answer: Start LibreOffice in server mode with: soffice --headless --accept='socket,host=localhost,port=8100;urp;'. Then use Python with the uno library to connect.

faq.question: What is the best format for archival?
faq.answer: ODS is recommended for archival as it is an open standard, well-documented, and preserves all spreadsheet features including formulas and formatting.

faq.question: Can macros be preserved in conversions?
faq.answer: No, macros (VBA or LibreOffice Basic) are not preserved when converting between formats. You will need to recreate macros in the target format.
